summary: This is a brief announcement post with two unrelated items. First, Joel promotes his appearance at the Microsoft Professional Developer Conference in Los Angeles, where he'll be signing books and giving away copies of FogBUGZ at a booth. Second, he shares a Bruce Schneier quote arguing against terror profiling by using the provocative example of flagging all U.S. ex-servicemen as potential terrorists.
key_points: Joel was actively promoting FogBUGZ and his books at industry conferences like Microsoft's PDC
He shared Bruce Schneier's argument that terror profiling is fundamentally flawed by illustrating how absurd broad-category surveillance becomes when applied to sympathetic groups
The post reflects the post-9/11 discourse around security profiling that was prominent in 2003
money_quotes: I think we need to put all U.S. ex-servicemen on a special watch list, because they obviously could be terrorists.
I hope you're appalled, incensed and angry…
key_insight: This is a lightweight announcement post combining conference self-promotion with a shared link on the ineffectiveness of terror profiling.
